title: Popover
description: Anchored floating surface for contextual content (non-modal). Consider using the HTML Popover API where available.

props.name: dismissible
props.description: Whether outside click and Esc can close the popover.

props.name: modal
props.description: When true, behaves more like a dialog (use sparingly).

states.name: open
effects: Content is positioned and layered above other content.

states.name: focus-visible
states.description: Keyboard focus indicator on trigger or content elements.
effects: Show focus ring using semantic border focus token.

keyboard: Esc closes if dismissible is true and returns focus to trigger.
Tab should move naturally; do not trap focus unless modal is true.
aria: If content is a simple region, use aria-expanded on trigger and aria-controls pointing to content.
If the popover behaves like a dialog (modal=true), use role="dialog" and an accessible name.

must: Popover must not obscure critical content without a way to dismiss it.
Focus management must be predictable: on close, return focus to trigger.
should: Close on outside click when dismissible is true.
Support repositioning to stay within viewport.
must_not: Do not use popovers for essential information that must always be visible (use inline).
Do not trap focus in a non-modal popover.

token_usage.notes: Use surface background for floating content.
Use overlay shadow for floating surfaces.
Use surface radius for the popover panel.
Use UI motion token for transitions.

examples.title: Trigger + region skeleton
examples.html: Contextual content…
